name: The Golden Rule
description: The principle of treating others as one wishes to be treated, found across all major religious traditions.

passages.text: Thou shalt not take vengeance, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself: I am the Lord.
Therefore, whatever you desire for others to do to you, you shall also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets.
He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate, who is free from egoism and the sense of 'mine,' who is equal in pain and pleasure, who is forgiving—such a devotee is dear to Me.
All tremble before violence. All fear death. Putting oneself in the place of another, one should not kill nor cause another to kill.

name: Compassion & Empathy
description: The virtue of showing kindness, understanding, and mercy toward others, especially those who suffer.

passages.text: Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world. By love alone is hatred appeased. This is an ancient truth.
The recompense for an injury is an injury equal thereto, but whoever forgives and makes reconciliation, his reward is with Allah. Indeed, He does not like wrongdoers.
He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate, who is free from egoism... such a devotee is dear to Me.
Love is patient and is kind. Love doesn't envy. Love doesn't brag, is not proud.
He who looks upon all with a single eye, and knows all as part of the One—that person is truly wise.

name: Purpose of Life
description: Teachings about the meaning, goal, and purpose of human existence.

passages.text: Perform your prescribed duties, for action is superior to inaction. Even the maintenance of your body would not be possible through inaction.
Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: fear God, and keep his commandments; for this is the whole duty of man.
I created the jinn and humankind only that they might worship Me.
You yourselves must make the effort; the Buddhas only show the way. Those who practice the path and meditate will be released from the bonds of death.
There is One God, the Eternal Truth. He is the Creator, without fear and without hate... By the Guru's grace, He is realized.

name: Forgiveness
description: The act of pardoning others for wrongs committed and the divine forgiveness of sins.

passages.text: For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in knowledge and yoga, charity, self-control, sacrifice, study of scriptures, austerity, honesty—these are the qualities of the divine.
The recompense for an injury is an injury equal thereto, but whoever forgives and makes reconciliation, his reward is with Allah.
Conquer anger by love; conquer evil by good; conquer the stingy by giving; conquer the liar by truth.

name: Afterlife & Salvation
description: Beliefs about what happens after death, including heaven, hell, rebirth, and liberation.

passages.text: For the soul there is neither birth nor death at any time. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain.
For God so loved the world, that he gave his only born Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
His command is only when He intends a thing that He says to it, 'Be,' and it is. So exalted is He in whose hand is the dominion of all things, and to Him you will be returned.
Earnestness is the path of immortality, thoughtlessness the path of death. Those who are earnest do not die; those who are thoughtless are as if dead already.
Blissful is the state of the one who sings the Lord's praises. I am in ecstasy, listening to the divine melody. The Lord's name is ambrosial; drinking it, all thirst is quenched.
I call heaven and earth to witness against you this day, that I have set before thee life and death, the blessing and the curse; therefore choose life, that thou mayest live, thou and thy seed.

name: Charity & Generosity
description: The virtue of giving to those in need, including almsgiving, charity, and service to others.

passages.text: Righteousness is [in] one who... gives wealth, in spite of love for it, to relatives, orphans, the needy, the traveler, those who ask [for help], and for freeing slaves.
Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
That gift which is given without expectation of return, at the right time and place, to a worthy person—that gift is pure.
If there is among you a poor man... you shall not harden your heart nor shut your hand from your poor brother; but you shall open your hand wide to him.
The True Name is my support and my treasure; it removes all my sorrows and fulfills all my needs.

name: Peace & Non-Violence
description: Teachings about peace, non-violence, and resolving conflicts without harm.

passages.text: All tremble before violence. All fear death. Putting oneself in the place of another, one should not kill nor cause another to kill.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.
He whose mind is untroubled in the midst of sorrows and is free from eager desire amid pleasures, he from whom passion, fear, and rage have passed away—he is called a sage of steady wisdom.

O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you.
If I please Him, I am blessed. The True Guru has united me with Him. The word of the Guru is the inner music; hearing it, I find peace.
The Lord bless thee, and keep thee; the Lord make His face to shine upon thee, and be gracious unto thee; the Lord lift up His countenance upon thee, and give thee peace.

name: Wisdom & Knowledge
description: The pursuit of wisdom, understanding, and spiritual knowledge as a path to truth.

passages.text: Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don't lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.
If you see an intelligent person who detects faults and blames what is blameworthy, follow that wise person as you would a guide to hidden treasure.

Whenever there is a decline of righteousness and a rise of unrighteousness, I manifest Myself. For the protection of the good, for the destruction of the wicked, and for the establishment of righteousness, I come into being age after age.
Truth is higher than everything, but higher still is true living. Without the True Guru, no one finds the way.
There shall be no compulsion in religion. The right course has become clear from the wrong.

name: Justice & Righteousness
description: The principles of fairness, justice, and living righteously according to divine law.

passages.text: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm for Allah, witnesses in justice, and do not let the hatred of a people prevent you from being just. Be just; that is nearer to righteousness.
Justice, justice shalt thou pursue, that thou mayest live, and inherit the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
He has shown you, O man, what is good. What does Yahweh require of you, but to act justly, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God?
Abandon all varieties of dharmas and surrender unto Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.
Let go of anger; abandon pride. Transcend every worldly attachment. No suffering befalls one who is not attached to name and form.

name: Faith & Trust in God
description: The role of faith, trust, and belief in the divine as a foundation for spiritual life.

passages.text: To those who worship Me with devotion, meditating on Me, I give what they lack and preserve what they have.
Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained.
Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is One. And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.
There is One God, the Eternal Truth. He is the Creator, without fear and without hate. He is timeless and without form.

name: Humility & Service
description: The virtue of humility, putting others before oneself, and serving others as a spiritual practice.

passages.text: He by whom the world is not troubled and who is not troubled by the world, who is free from joy and envy, fear and anxiety—he is dear to Me.
Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
One is one's own refuge; what other refuge could there be? With self well-tamed, one obtains a refuge hard to find.
Why do you go to the forest in search of the Divine? He dwells in all, yet remains distinct... Seek Him in your own heart.

name: Suffering & Endurance
description: Teachings on the nature of suffering, its causes, and how to endure and transcend it.

passages.text: Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with an impure mind a person speaks or acts, suffering follows him like the wheel that follows the foot of the ox.
He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain any more. The first things have passed away.
He whose mind is untroubled in the midst of sorrows and is free from eager desire amid pleasures... he is called a sage of steady wisdom.
For indeed, with hardship comes ease. Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me.

name: Love & Devotion
description: The concept of divine love, loving God, and the importance of love in spiritual life.

passages.text: But now faith, hope, and love remain—these three. The greatest of these is love.
Ask forgiveness of your Lord, then turn to Him in repentance. Indeed, my Lord is Most Merciful, Most Loving.
Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer, whatever you give away, whatever austerities you perform—do that as an offering unto Me.
Blissful is the state of the one who sings the Lord's praises. I am in ecstasy, listening to the divine melody.
And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.
